findings.title: Fork helpers expose RPC credentials in process arguments by default
findings.description: `fame-swap:fork` and `fame-swap:local-dev` set `FAME_SWAP_ALLOW_RPC_URL_PROCESS_ARG` to `1` when it is unset, so `fame-swap-fork-smoke.ts` skips the loopback proxy and spawns Anvil with `--fork-url ${BASE_RPC_URL || NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_RPC_URL_1}`. If that URL contains an Alchemy/Infura token, it is readable from the Anvil command line for the lifetime of the fork by local process listing or CI process collectors.
findings.recommendation: Do not default the wrapper scripts to `FAME_SWAP_ALLOW_RPC_URL_PROCESS_ARG=1`; let the existing loopback proxy be the default and require an explicit opt-in when the URL is safe to expose as a process argument.

findings.title: Readiness errors are returned without diagnostic redaction
findings.description: The public quote endpoint performs live readiness reads with the configured Base RPC URL, and `liveReadiness` appends the raw caught `error.message` to `readiness.message`. For non-ready quotes the route returns both `message` and `readiness` to the unauthenticated caller, so a provider/network error that includes the request URL or API-token-bearing endpoint is reflected in `/api/fame/swap/quote` responses. The live quote adapter has URL/hex redaction, but this readiness path bypasses it.
findings.recommendation: Apply the same display-safe redaction used by live quote diagnostics before adding readiness read errors to `readiness.message`, or return a fixed public readiness failure and keep provider details server-side only.
